excluded_paths.reason: Local validation output can contain mirrored scanner findings and is not source.
Local Skylos cache/state is not source.
Build tree generated by native toolchains.
Fixture PDFs are test inputs, not executable source.
Downloaded model files are external runtime data.
Vendored dependency/reference repositories are explicitly out of Skylos scope.
Generated OpenAPI output from the Rust utoipa exporter.

accepted_findings.reason: TanStack Router consumes route modules by file convention through the generated route tree.
Storybook consumes CSF stories as build-time entrypoints.
Storybook CSF exports are discovered by Storybook rather than imported by application code.
Storybook loads preview/main config files directly.
Storybook consumes the default-exported config object at build time.
TanStack Router discovers route modules and their exported Route symbols by file convention.
Current React surfaces are covered by Bun tests/Storybook; component split work is tracked by SCC and client lint gates.
Workbench state orchestration is tested and kept under file-level SCC limits; split only when behavior changes.
False positive for framework entrypoints and feature modules referenced through generated routing or public barrel exports.
Cell renderers are exported for TanStack table column definitions and Storybook iteration.
Packaging/runtime scripts are platform orchestration entrypoints; validated by py_compile and unittest while longer-term splits are tracked by SCC.
Tests use explicit scenario setup for readability and regression coverage.
Runtime downloads are restricted by validate_github_repo and validate_github_url to HTTPS GitHub release hosts.
Reads are fixed release manifests or downloaded artifacts under safe_child_path-bounded installer directories.
Install and download paths are rooted through safe_child_path, and archive members are traversal-checked before extraction.
PP-OCRv6 model manifests and downloaded model files are written under the operator-selected runtime output directory after fixed host and file-size validation.
PP-OCRv6 model reads use the fixed embedded-ocr bundle shape or the fixed Hugging Face manifest declared in the installer.
PP-OCRv6 staging paths are derived from the installer output root and fixed model module/file names before copy or download.

PaddleOCR-VL layout staging uses the fixed embedded-ocr manifest, HTTPS Hugging Face host allowlisting, file size and SHA-256 verification, and writes only under the operator-selected output directory.
Tesseract staging copies a discovered system/build binary and tessdata into fixed children of the runtime output directory.
Setup output paths are derived from resolve_portable_root, which canonicalizes a Trapo repo containing pyproject.toml.
Runtime environment parsing uses fixed uv/python commands and exports only allowlisted installer JSON keys.
Packaging reads fixed repo manifests, release artifacts, and GitHub-provided env files in CI-controlled locations.
The runtime engine guard reads fixed repo manifests, workflow YAML, runtime sidecars, and release archives produced by CI or local quality gates.
Native OCR dependency downloads are HTTPS host-allowlisted, SHA256-pinned, traversal-checked before extraction, and staged only into operator-selected build/output directories.
Native runner staging copies fixed Cargo release binaries into the runtime package staging directory selected by CI or the local release operator.
PDFium downloads are built from the fixed pdfium-binaries repo and checked by validate_pdfium_url.
Packaged workbench smoke requests are restricted to the local 127.0.0.1 server started by the same script.
Archive members are traversal-checked before extraction, and smoke logs are written under the temporary extracted app root.
Tar members are bounded to the temporary extraction root and extracted with Python's data filter.
Delete/copy operations target deterministic package stage children and archive outputs under the configured output directory.
Launcher, manifest, README, and checksum writes use deterministic paths inside the package staging root.
The triage wrapper reads operator-selected local Skylos JSON artifacts and the repo-local exception registry.
The triage wrapper writes operator-selected local reports under docs/skylos/issues or ignored .logs paths.
The quality runner reads and writes operator-selected local gate logs/reports and GitHub Actions summary/annotation output.
The ABI test reads an operator-selected local runtime library to verify exported symbols.
All generated API clients pass through toApiPath, which only permits same-origin /api/ paths.
fileHash/file_hash values are public document identifiers used for UI routing, not secret tokens.
file_hash and run_id values are public workbench route and run membership identifiers used to group explorer nodes, not secret tokens.
file_hash values are public document identifiers used to match diagnostic work units to explorer engine status nodes, not secret tokens.
file_hash and run_id values are public run membership identifiers used to choose explorer filter targets, not secret tokens.
fileHash/file_hash values are public document identifiers used for UI auto-follow state, not secret tokens.
file_hash values are public document identifiers used for cache projection, not secret tokens.
fileHash values are public document identifiers used for UI selection, not secret tokens.
fileHash values are public document identifiers used for OCR focus state, not secret tokens.
Replay pages are cursor-dependent; each /api/ocr/events request must wait for next_since_sequence before requesting the next page.
Build script paths are derived from Cargo OUT_DIR and CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR.
Document paths come from discover_supported_files after validate_trusted_root accepts a local ingest root.
Annotation identity drafts contain OCR source keys and normalized bounding boxes for DuckDB persistence; they are not appended to filesystem paths.
The OpenAPI exporter writes an explicit developer-supplied CLI output path.
Command values are fixed hardware probe literals, not request-controlled executable names.
Browser opener commands are fixed per OS and receive only the local server URL.
The log file is opened under the app log root configured during local server startup.
The log and raw stdio capture files are opened under the app log root configured during local server startup.
The repository opens the configured DuckDB file under the app data directory during local server startup; request handlers do not choose this path.
The manifest guard reads DB.md and repository source files derived from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR to enforce query coverage; request or network input cannot choose these paths.
FFI paths are selected from local runtime/model configuration before the unsafe loader boundary.
Native OCR image reads use page render paths produced by the trusted ingest/render pipeline before passing bytes into the selected in-process OCR library.
Embedding worker request and response JSON files are hidden internal IPC paths generated under a canonical temp worker directory or canonicalized before child-worker read/write.
PDF/image inputs and rendered page outputs are local ingest artifacts under validated roots.
Rust module declarations and reexports are resolved by cargo/rustc; cargo clippy is authoritative for unused imports.
utoipa derives consume schema helper functions through macro expansion.
Axum/utoipa route handlers are registered through router composition and macro expansion.
Server payload DTOs are consumed through Serde, Axum extractors/responses, utoipa schemas, and generated client contracts; cargo clippy and integration tests are authoritative for Rust use.
App include files share a single Rust module; helper context structs are consumed across included items that Skylos does not resolve.
AppState route methods are called from Axum route modules and included implementation fragments; cargo clippy and API integration tests validate reachability.
Small app helpers are referenced from Rust include fragments; cargo clippy is authoritative for actual dead code.
The shutdown task registry is called from AppState include fragments that Skylos does not resolve; cargo clippy and shutdown route tests validate reachability.
FFI helper functions are shared across included native ABI fragments that Skylos does not resolve.
The packaged runtime smoke command reaches the validator through the parent embedding module re-export; cargo clippy validates actual Rust reachability.
The hidden --embedding-worker CLI reaches this function through embedding and library re-exports; process_logging integration tests validate the child process path.
Repository methods are reached through async application services across Rust include fragments; cargo clippy and storage tests validate reachability.
The flagged file_hash assignment stores a content digest identity in DuckDB RAG segment rows, not a filesystem path or filesystem sink.
Embedding profiles canonicalize the model file under the configured model directory and canonicalize the llama.cpp library before the FFI session opens.
Pre-commit is optional for this repo; CI and local quality commands are the required gates.
